funders.name: Central Florida Foundation - 100 Women Strong
funders.why_fit: Giving circle specifically funds programs improving the lives of women and children in Central Florida; aligns with EGI's girls mentorship and empowerment mission.
funders.eligibility_notes: 501(c)(3) organizations serving Central Florida. Applications typically reviewed by member vote; prior grantees include youth and workforce development nonprofits.
funders.deadline: Rolling / annual giving circle cycle (contact foundation for current LOI timeline)

funders.name: Orlando Magic Youth Foundation
funders.why_fit: Core mission is underserved youth in Central Florida; micro and major grant programs open to local nonprofits running mentorship, after-school, and youth development programs.
funders.eligibility_notes: Local 501(c)(3) organizations focused on youth. Both major grants and micro grants available; profit status required.
funders.deadline: Multiple cycles per year (summer and winter cycles; check site for open dates)

funders.name: Publix Super Markets Charities
funders.why_fit: Publix Charities prioritizes youth and education initiatives in communities where Publix operates, making EGI's Orlando-based girls mentorship a strong fit.
funders.eligibility_notes: Must be an established 501(c)(3); youth and education focus. Non-monetary requests also considered.
funders.deadline: Rolling, though many awards are coordinated regionally; early submission recommended.

funders.name: City of Orlando Community Investment Grant Program
funders.why_fit: Direct local government grant stream supporting nonprofits delivering programs that strengthen Orlando neighborhoods and serve youth.
funders.eligibility_notes: Organizations must serve City of Orlando residents and maintain a nonprofit profile in Central Florida Nonprofit Search.
funders.deadline: Annual cycle with set application windows; verify current year dates on site.

funders.name: CareerSource Central Florida
funders.geography: Central Florida (Orange, Osceola, Seminole, Lake, Sumter counties)
funders.why_fit: Administers federal and state workforce funding that supports youth training, mentorship, and career-readiness programs—directly applicable to EGI's mentorship and skill-building goals.
funders.eligibility_notes: Nonprofit and education partners can apply for specific youth programs; must align with WIOA youth eligibility and local workforce priorities.
funders.deadline: Program-specific solicitations; annual plan available online.

funders.name: Central Florida Women's League Foundation
funders.why_fit: Known for awarding grants to community nonprofits and scholarships to young women; mission closely overlaps with EGI's target population and goals.
funders.eligibility_notes: 501(c)(3) organizations and scholarship programs serving Central Florida; formal application required.
funders.deadline: Annual grant cycle; verify current deadline via website contact form.

funders.name: VyStar Foundation
funders.why_fit: Funds youth empowerment and career counseling programs across Florida, including recent youth nonprofit grants that align with EGI's mentorship model.
funders.eligibility_notes: Open to nonprofits within VyStar service areas; youth and education focus preferred.
funders.deadline: Annual grant cycles and occasional rapid-response awards; check website for current solicitations.

partners.name: Boys & Girls Clubs of Central Florida
partners.geography: Central Florida (15 counties, strong Orlando presence)
partners.why_fit: Large youth-serving network with existing infrastructure, staff, and facilities; shared interest in girls' mentorship and after-school enrichment.
partners.collaboration_opportunities: Co-host girls-only mentorship tracks at Club sites, share volunteer mentor pools, cross-promote programming, and jointly apply for youth workforce/education grants.

partners.name: Orange County Public Schools
partners.why_fit: Direct access to girls in middle and high school—the core audience for EGI's mentorship and empowerment programming—through existing school structures.
partners.collaboration_opportunities: In-school mentorship sessions, parent-night presentations, referral pipeline into programming, shared use of school facilities for workshops.

partners.name: Orange County Library System
partners.why_fit: Libraries are trusted community hubs that already run teen and girls programs; ideal venue and promotional partner for workshops, book clubs, and mentor meetups.
partners.collaboration_opportunities: Host EGI mentorship events and leadership workshops at library branches, co-sponsor teen summer series, cross-market through library event calendars and e-newsletters.

partners.name: Hispanic Chamber of Metro Orlando
partners.why_fit: Chamber networks connect nonprofits to business sponsors, mentors, and Latino/Hispanic families—broadening EGI's reach and potential mentor pipeline in Central Florida.
partners.collaboration_opportunities: Introduce EGI to member mentors and corporate sponsors, co-host leadership/networking events for young women, provide internship and job-shadow pathways.

partners.name: Valencia College
partners.geography: Orlando / Orange and Osceola counties, FL
partners.why_fit: Community colleges prioritize youth pathways and career coaching; can provide college-readiness components and campus-based mentor training or venues.
partners.collaboration_opportunities: Develop college-readiness modules for EGI mentees, co-design scholarship or dual-enrollment info sessions, use campus spaces for summer immersion programs.

partners.name: Orlando Health
partners.why_fit: Health system with community benefit and foundation arms; can lend wellness expertise, mental health speakers, and health-screening resources to a girls' mentorship program.
partners.collaboration_opportunities: Provide health and wellness workshops (nutrition, stress management, reproductive health), sponsor program events, connect mentees with healthcare career shadowing.

partners.name: Prospera
partners.why_fit: Bilingual economic development nonprofit serving Hispanic families and entrepreneurs; offers complementary empowerment angle and access to bilingual mentors and families.
partners.collaboration_opportunities: Bilingual co-facilitation, referral network for Spanish-speaking mentees and families, joint small-business or financial-literacy workshops for girls.

resources.name: TechSoup
resources.why_fit: Primary connector for discounted or donated technology products and software for U.S. 501(c)(3)s; EGI can procure hardware and software at reduced cost.
resources.eligibility_notes: Must be a recognized 501(c)(3) in good standing. Product availability varies; registration required. Some items have quantity or budget restrictions.

resources.name: Google for Nonprofits
resources.why_fit: Provides free or discounted Google Workspace, Google Ad Grants, and YouTube tools—critical for a small nonprofit managing donor outreach, scheduling, and communications.
resources.eligibility_notes: U.S. 501(c)(3) organizations with a valid EIN and a matching domain for account verification are eligible.

resources.name: Microsoft for Nonprofits
resources.why_fit: Offers donated and discounted Microsoft 365, cloud credits, and productivity tools—helping EGI operate securely and professionally on a nonprofit budget.
resources.eligibility_notes: U.S. 501(c)(3) and similar charitable organizations. Some offers donated via TechSoup; other subscriptions discounted directly.
resources.deadline: Rolling eligibility; product availability continues indefinitely.

resources.name: Canva for Nonprofits
resources.why_fit: Free Canva Pro access supports fast, branded creation of flyers, lesson materials, social media, reports, and fundraising materials without a designer.
resources.eligibility_notes: Qualifying 501(c)(3) or similar nonprofit. Application verified by TechSoup or Canva; approved users receive Canva for Nonprofits team access.

resources.name: Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud (Power of Us Program)
resources.why_fit: Free or deeply discounted CRM and nonprofit-specific cloud edition helps track donors, mentors, mentees, program outcomes, and volunteer hours at scale.
resources.eligibility_notes: 501(c)(3) or equivalent. Requires Salesforce.org application and verification; some add-on products remain discounted rather than free.

resources.name: AmeriCorps through Volunteer Florida
resources.why_fit: Provides national-service member placements to nonprofits, delivering dedicated program capacity and a living-stipend-supported workforce at low cost to EGI.
resources.eligibility_notes: Nonprofit/host organizations must be selected via competitive formula or subgrant for AmeriCorps member placements; sponsor organization requirements apply.
resources.deadline: Opportunity announcements tied to annual AmeriCorps funding cycles; check current solicitations.

resources.name: University of Central Florida - Nonprofit Capacity Building Program
resources.why_fit: UCF offers free or low-cost capacity-building training and one-on-one technical assistance to Orange County nonprofits—ideal for strengthening EGI's operations and grant readiness.
resources.eligibility_notes: Typically limited to Orange County nonprofits; cohort-based selection and competitive application for technical assistance slots.

resources.name: National Council of Nonprofits
resources.why_fit: Free resources, training, and advocacy guidance for nonprofits; helps EGI stay compliant, improve governance, and access best-practice toolkits without cost.
resources.eligibility_notes: Open access to many online resources; some premium member benefits require organizational membership.
